articles.full_text:
Home |Hyderabad |Telangana Heatwave Mercury To Hit 44 Degree Celsius As Dry Phase Sets In From Saturday
Telangana heatwave: Mercury to hit 44 degree Celsius as dry phase sets in from Saturday
Heatwave conditions are set to continue across Telangana with temperatures ranging between 40°C and 44°C. While isolated thunderstorms may bring brief relief on Friday, a dry spell is expected from Saturday, intensifying heat across northern and western districts.
Hyderabad: Heatwave conditions will continue to prevail across all districts of Telangana on Friday, with maximum temperatures expected to range from 40 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.
While sporadic spells of thunderstorms, lightning, accompanied by gusty winds, may provide brief relief, the daytime heat is expected to be brutal, thanks to intense dry conditions during the afternoons.
In Hyderabad and its surrounding districts including Sangareddy, Vikarabad, Rangareddy and Medchal-Malkajgiri, the maximum day time temperatures are expected to hover at around 40 degree Celsius, according to India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad forecast.
The dry heat during the ongoing heatwave conditions are expected to be particularly harsh in northern and western districts of Telangana, with maximum temperatures ranging between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius. Already, the mercury is consistently touching 44 degree Celsius in northern districts like Adilabad and Nizamabad and Eastern districts like Nalgonda.
While IMD, Hyderabad has continued with sporadic spells of thunderstorms on Friday at different locations, from Saturday onwards, the State is entering into a strictly dry phase, with no chance of thundershowers, lightning and gusty winds.

articles.title: Heatwave intensifies across Telangana with temperatures touching 45°C - Hyderabad Mail
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: A severe heatwave gripped several parts of Telangana on the first day of the “Heatwave 2.0” alert, with temperatures soaring up to 44–45°C in northern districts, according to initial weather updates.
Districts including Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad (Asifabad), Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Mancherial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Kamareddy, Peddapalli, Mulugu and Bhadradri Kothagudem recorded maximum temperatures in the range of 44–45°C.
Several other regions, including Karimnagar, Nalgonda, Khammam, Suryapet, Jogulamba Gadwal, Wanaparthy, Siddipet and Warangal, witnessed slightly lower but still severe temperatures between 43–44°C.
The state capital, Hyderabad, recorded temperatures ranging from 41–43°C, indicating intensifying heat conditions in urban areas as well.
Meteorological trends suggest that interior and northern Telangana districts are experiencing the brunt of the heatwave, with temperatures remaining significantly above seasonal averages.
Authorities are expected to issue advisories urging residents to avoid direct sun exposure during peak hours and to take precautions against heat-related illnesses.

articles.title: Heatwave Alert Issued for Telangana as Temperatures Set to Soar Above 44°C - Telangana Tribune
articles.full_text:
Telangana is bracing for another round of intense heatwave conditions as meteorological authorities have issued a Heatwave 2.0 alert for multiple districts across the state. Several districts are expected to experience scorching temperatures ranging between 44-45°C, prompting concerns for public health and safety. The alert marks the beginning of what could be another challenging period for residents as summer intensifies across the region.
The highest temperatures between 44-45°C are forecasted for Adilabad, Asifabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagitial, Mancherial, Bhupalapally, Kamareddy, Peddapalli, Mulugu, and Bhadradri-Kothagudem districts. These areas are expected to bear the brunt of the extreme heat conditions during this phase of the heatwave. Meanwhile, Karimnagar, Nalgonda, Khammam, Suryapet, Gadwal, Wanaparthy, Siddipet, and Warangal districts are likely to witness temperatures between 43-44°C, creating uncomfortable and potentially dangerous conditions for outdoor activities.
The state capital Hyderabad is also not spared from the severe heat, with temperatures expected to range between 41-43°C. The mercury levels in the city are set to cause significant discomfort to residents, particularly those working outdoors or without access to adequate cooling facilities. The heatwave conditions are expected to affect daily life across the capital region, with authorities monitoring the situation closely.
Health officials typically advise residents to stay indoors during peak afternoon hours, remain hydrated, and avoid direct sun exposure during heatwave conditions. Vulnerable populations including children, elderly citizens, and outdoor workers face heightened risks during such extreme temperature events. The alert serves as a crucial warning for districts across Telangana to prepare for the intense heat conditions expected in the coming days.
